entry.title: Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
entry.content:
Abstract

This study guide is designed to assist you in preparing for the SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (P_C4H34_2411). This certification verifies that you possess the proficiency needed to work effectively with the SAP Commerce Cloud Suite. Catering to a professional/expert level audience, this certification demands candidates demonstrate technical skills and a deep understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud functionalities. With three years of practical experience being a prerequisite, the certification addresses complex problem-solving scenarios.

If you are preparing for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (C_C4H32_2411), please check out this other dedicated study guide.

Before we start

To prepare for the certification, it's highly recommended to engage in comprehensive training sessions and/or online learning platforms. The below options provide structured learning paths to develop the necessary skills:

  • Instructor-led Training:
    • C4H340 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
    • C4H341 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
  • E-Learning Courses: For those unable to attend training sessions in person, e-learning options are available with equivalent content.
    • Beginner: Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Intermediate: Expand Upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Advanced: Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Hands-on Practice Systems: These trainings and e-learning offerings are complemented by dedicated practice systems, allowing you to perform hands-on exercises with SAP Commerce Cloud. This practical experience is designed to enhance your understanding, with PDFs of the training materials accessible in the exercise environments. Specifically:
    • The following practice systems correspond to the mentioned trainings:
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
    • Additionally, the practice systems related to the three e-learning courses include:
      • Hands-on Practice for Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
      • Hands-on Practice for Expanding Upon SAP Commerce Cloud Technical Essentials
      • Hands-on Practice for Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
         
  • Certification Live Session - SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer Certification Preparation: This live session features an instructor who reviews key exam topics and can directly address many of your related questions.

But please note: These resources provide a comprehensive foundation but aren't exhaustive for examination readiness due to the certification's professional level.

The certification exam is categorized into the 10 topic areas outlined below:

  • Platform Basics
  • Platform Features
  • PCM (Product Content Management)
  • Commerce
  • Customer Experience
  • Order Management and Customer Services
  • Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal
  • Backoffice Framework
  • Integration
  • Managing Clean Core

Let's explore each topic area in detail.

Walkthrough of All Topics

1. Platform Basics (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should focus on the following objectives:

  • Comprehend the Ant build framework and essential configuration files.
  • Explain the concept of extensions and manage them effectively, including property files and dependencies.
  • Describe data modeling concepts and the purpose of 'items.xml', including item types, primary key generation, relationships/collections, enumerations, maps, dynamic attributes, and database type deployment.
  • Manage security aspects related to type-based access rights and restrictions.
  • Master writing flexible search queries.
  • Understand ImpEx syntax, how to execute ImpEx, and its scripting support.
  • Identify the features and implementation of service-oriented architecture and the service layer, including model objects, monitoring via interceptors, and management using 'modelService'.

Important Links:

  • Building SAP Commerce
  • Extension Concept
  • Type System
  • ServiceLayer
  • Users in the SAP Commerce Platform
  • Search restrictions
  • Spring Framework in SAP Commerce
  • Search Mechanisms
  • Data Management with ImpEx
  • ImpEx API

2. Platform Features (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should aim to:

  • Understand the event framework, including special event types like 'ClusterAwareEvent', 'TransactionAwareEvent', and 'AfterSaveEvent'.
  • Manage CronJobs by working with data models, implementation steps, and aborting methods.
  • Identify caching features, such as the framework, eviction policies, configuration options, and clustering.
  • Explain the data conversion and population process.
  • Import data using the system setup utility and patch classes.
  • Understand the implementation and customization of Omni Commerce Connect (OCC).
  • Identify the functionalities of workflows, workflow templates, and the collaboration center.
  • Understand scripting support and its applications.

Important Links:

  • Event System
  • The Cron Job Service
  • Cache
  • Converters and Populators
  • Workflow and Collaboration
  • Omni Commerce Connect
  • Scripting Engine

3. PCM (Product Content Management) (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should aim to understand the following:

  • Product and catalog management, including products, categories, catalog versions, and synchronization management.
  • Different product modeling approaches, such as basic variants, multi-dimensional variants, and the classification system.
  • Media management, focusing on elements like media containers, media formats, and synchronization.

Important Links:

  • Product Modelling
  • Classification
  • Working with Catalogs
  • Synchronizing Catalogs
  • Media
  • Muiti-Dimensional variants

4. Commerce (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the search and navigation module, encompassing backoffice configuration, indexed types, index queries/jobs/operations, indexed properties, facet and facet range configuration, as well as search services and listeners.
  • Identify the features of the adaptive search module, including its configuration, search profiles, and the boosting of items and rules.
  • Manage and customize the rule engine-based promotion module, covering out-of-the-box promotions, conditions and actions, rule-aware objects, and coupon types.
  • Explain the pricing mechanism, focusing on price rows and their decoupling from catalogs.
  • Understand basic stock management, including data models, the stock service, and display on storefronts.
  • Identify cart management and the implementation of the commerce cart service.
  • Recognize the relationship between BaseStore, Catalogs, and Warehouses.

Important Links:

  • Search And Navigation Module
  • Adaptive Search Module
  • Promotion
  • Price, Tax, and Discount Calculation
  • Commerce Service Module

5. Customer Experience (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Identify the out-of-the-box CMS item types, including base store, base site, template, page, content slot, and component, as well as their type hierarchy.
  • Implement and manage custom CMS component types and CMS restrictions.
  • Recognize the key features of SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront.
  • Understand the features and management of CMS content using SmartEdit, including various modes such as preview, basic/advanced edit, versioning, and navigation management.
  • Comprehend the personalization module, including related data models, calculation processes, voters, and commerce customization for personalized search and promotions.

Important Links:

  • SmartEdit
  • WCMS Module
  • Personalization module
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront

6. Order Management and Customer Services (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the basecommerce module and typical Order APIs, including splitting strategies, cancellations, returns, and refunds.
  • Manage existing and custom business processes, focusing on their concepts, node types, and custom implementation.
  • Identify the standard checkout and fulfillment processes.
  • Describe the features of the Assisted Service Module and Customer Service Module.
  • Understand the Order Management Services Module.

Important Links:

  • Base Commerce Module
  • Order Management Services Module
  • Assisted Service Module
  • Customer Service Module
  • Business Process Management

7. Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain the architecture of SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • Understand how to set up the code repository.
  • Describe how to configure 'manifest.json' files, manage environments, and configure endpoints.
  • Explain how to connect to the code repository and how to build and deploy your application on the Cloud Portal.

Important Links:

  • Build Manifest Components
  • Cloud Portal
  • Cloud Automation Components
  • Project Customizations

8. Backoffice Framework (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Describe how to create and manage backoffice applications using the Backoffice framework.
    • This includes managing widgets, configuring connections, utilizing the application orchestrator, implementing dynamic forms, and using virtual sockets, among other features.

Important Links:

  • Backoffice Framework Module, especially:
    • Widgets
    • UI Configuration Overview
    • Dynamic Forms in UI Configuration
    • Virtual Sockets
  • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice Framework Developer

9. Integration (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain how to apply, manage, and monitor Integration APIs and Webhooks within the Backoffice.
  • Identify the features and functionalities of Cloud Hot Folders.
  • Recognize the basic features of Intelligent Selling Services and the SAP CX AI Toolkit for SAP Commerce Cloud.

Important Links:

  • Integration API Module
  • Cloud Hot Folders
  • Webhooks
  • Intelligent Selling Services
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

10. Managing Clean Core (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the concept of Clean Core and its essential components.
  • Identify the application of Clean Core across various dimensions, including Business Processes, Extensions, Data, Landscapes, and Operations.

Important Links:

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, especially:
    • Exploring How to Make Landscapes Core Compliant
    • Exploring How to Make Operations Core Compliant

Summary

This study guide sets a strong foundation by outlining crucial topic areas to focus on along with recommended learning resources. Equip yourself with both theoretical knowledge and practical insight to excel in the certification exam.

Happy learning and good luck with your preparation!

entry.title: Value Input in Operations of Production Process
entry.content:
Dear All.

In the SAP public cloud, the customer requires a provision in the production process to input values during operations, and this data should also be included in the reporting. Is it possible to create such a provision within the standard process, or are there alternative solutions that can ensure the value input remains transparent throughout the process and is reflected in the reporting?

Please Reply

entry.title: Re: com.sap.it.rt.scc.connectivity.security.IToken.getPrincipalToken() - null value for 'tokenValue'
entry.content: got any solution?

entry.title: Re: Setup Time from Routing is used although Setup Catego...
entry.content: Check if Setup Matrix is assigned to the Resource

entry.title: Error when contingent worker is hired as permanent employee (SF-AD integration)
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing an issue with one of our new hires in the ActiveDirectory-SuccessFactors integration. 

Following message is shown in AD: 

Failure Description

An entry cannot not be provisioned in this target: SuccessFactors. The entry is 12345. The reason the entry cannot be provisioned is internal to that application. An example of why an entry cannot be provisioned is a user with a given public identifier, such as an email address, having previously exercised a regulatory right to be removed from the application.
ReportableIdentifier
emp.emp@company.com

The username of the previous employment was the username AD is trying to update now. emp.emp@company.com

 

The username of the new employment was a number sequence, like 12345. 

 

AD tried to update the new employment with the old username which was not possible since the old username was taken. 

 

From past experience, I have seen that the old username is automatically updated to username_INACT. Why does this not happen in this case? 

 

Hope someone knows the answer to this. I thought that was standard behavior. 

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: Hello Navi, you stated "I have same issue. Custom entity comes to list but cds views are not. I copied one of the listed CDS views (for example C_BOMMaterialVH to ZC_BOMMaterialVH) and gives C1 contract but still not listed." This is not a bug...currently one can only use Custom Entities as value help views for Key User Custom Fields. You can not use customer created view entities...even if you release them C1 for key users. We are aware of this gap and are already working on that. Best regards Georg

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service with camera
entry.content: Thank you for this blog. This is a great asset and an accelerator for our customers and partners explaining how to build apps for our Document Information Extraction service (DOX)! Looking forward to more posts around DOX.

entry.title: Cross entity business rule to make sure job and comp effective dates are the same
entry.content:
Hi,

Has anyone set up business rules to ensure that the dates in the Job Info and Comp Info portlets are the same? This is a requirement from one of my clients, and I would like to hear about your experience and how this was achieved before we proceed.

Appreciate all feedback

Br,

Ines

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content:
I read online that 404 could also be the response to an excessively large dataset, so I tried.

 

https://<tenant>.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/<space>/<am_id>/<am_id>(START_DATE=2023-12-29,END_DATE=2023-12-29)/Set?$select=0RPA_TNR

 

and I still get a 404. The result set is no more than 50 rows.

 

"Also I have variables with technical names such as "0CALDAY∞D0". How should I used those in the URL structure? I'm not a DS expert and do not know what the" ∞D0" means or where it's coming from.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
Hi,

there is an easier way to do this:

  1. Preferences -> Configuration: Logon
  2. select "Expert Mode " On
  3. Press "Save" button
  4. Right-Click on a Node in Logon
  5. From context menu select "Repair"
  6. Dialog opens: "SAP UI Landscape Repair"
  7. Select "Reset includes list"
  8. Press "Execute"

 An you are done.

BR
Robert

entry.title: post a vendor invoice using the BAPI BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST
entry.content: I need to post a vendor invoice using the BAPI BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST including tax amount

entry.title: Re: Vendor missing inside PR created through MRP Run
entry.content: Hi Sanan, Being a production i cannot change this setting without approvals. And besides as per screenshot shared with Caetano, you can see one PR showing Vendor and the other one without Vendor. Hence i think this shouldn't be the cause.

entry.title: Re: How to change the billing status for an outbound deli...
entry.content:
Hi,

what about billing document creation.

That step is clearly missing.

Transaction VF01 or VF04

 

Kind regards

Robert

entry.title: Re: MRP isn't including Reciept Reservation(debit) after the last requirement
entry.content: This is the MRP bug, we had meet the same problem, MRP can't consider the 531/581 receipt reservation which is after the last requirement

entry.title: Re: Output Management in TM - BRF+ or PPF?
entry.content:
Hello @papa261190 

LE-TR (shipments) uses NACE for output processing. In contrast TM relies on PPF actions. 

BRF+ and PPF is not an alternative. They are complementary, they work together. BRF+ can be used to implement conditions for PPF. BRF+ is for PPF, what output conditions are for NACE.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to work with test cases and test plans in several...
entry.content: Hello Aj, Can I ask you some questions regarding the test cases? You say you can reuse the test cases within the project but if we have several scopes (one for each rollout) in the project and we want to assign the test case to the next scope instead, I don´t see how to do this? I want to reuse the same test case but change the scope and I want to follow up the results, for instance, by using the cards in the overview tile. Is this possible?

entry.title: Re: How to Retrieve Warehouse Items with Serial Numbers a...
entry.content:
Hi @Fiorella ,

I encountered a similar scenario where I needed to identify the supplier associated with a batch number.

To address this, I created the following view, which I incorporated into my main query to retrieve the supplier details for the batch number.

You can adapt this approach to work with serial numbers as well.

SELECT
DISTINCT T1."ItemCode",
T1."Quantity" AS "Quantity",
T4."DistNumber" AS "LabelBoxNo",
T3."DocNum" AS "ReceivingDocNum",
T3."DocDate" AS "ReceivingDate",
T3."CardCode",
T3."CardName" 
FROM OITL T0 
INNER JOIN ITL1 T1 ON T0."LogEntry" = T1."LogEntry" AND T0."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode" 
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T2."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode" 
INNER JOIN OPDN T3 ON T3."DocEntry" = T0."ApplyEntry" AND T3."ObjType" = T0."ApplyType" 
INNER JOIN PDN1 T5 ON T5."DocEntry" = T3."DocEntry" 
INNER JOIN OBTN T4 ON T4."ItemCode" = T1."ItemCode" AND T4."AbsEntry" = T1."MdAbsEntry" 
WHERE T0."LocCode" IN ('01','02','03') AND T5."TargetType" <> 21 AND T3."CANCELED" = 'N' 
ORDER BY T1."ItemCode",T4."DistNumber

I hope this provides you with some useful insights!

Thank you.

Manish

entry.title: Re: [Group Reporting] creating subitem categories
entry.content:
Hi,

from a technically point of view it is no problem to add new subitem categories in order to differentiate parts of the balance sheet items. and in former consolidation solution like EC-CS or SEM-BCS it was a kind of best practice in doing so.

But in FIN-CS you've some restrictions especcialy in relation to the transfer of universal journal data and especially the use of correction positings via postins level 0C (for instance you have to set up all subitems to all subitem categories and additionally limit them via max.selections in order to have the entire functionalities for 0C-postings). Furthermore you have to note some dependencies in relation to the behaviour of BCF and CT.

So, from my point of view it is still very beneficial to use different subitems (using the same source: transaction type) to structure and manage postings within the balance sheet. But there are some obstacles so far.

BR

entry.title: Re: RAP OData v4 Web-API metadata set nullable option
entry.content: Thank you TuncayKaraca, I knew it works in cloud environment, should have tested in our on-premise system...

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Web Intelligence to SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Yes it has been fixed with patch 800.

BO on top of Datasphere has still some limitations and bugs.

Main limitation being the external hierarchy/with directrory not supported.

Hope this helps

Maxime

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch KBETR field in price condition using formula routine
entry.content: Hi, you should select these tables in a formula; especially not prcd_elements; for the other ones you can enhance komk/Komp with the required information, for prcd_elements you have the xkomv

entry.title: Re: Is there any method can upload and post API bank stat...
entry.content:
Hi Cosmo, 
@Cosmo_cao 

Exactly, as Edrilan has already stated there's the general standard Bank Statement - Post API. 

Apart from that, there are also a few other alternatives on how to get bank statements into your system. For that, see Overview on Automatic Bank Statement Processing.

For more Japan-specific information on processing bank statements, see Processing Bank Statements. 

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Enumerations and typed dimensions in SAP DM stack 2200
entry.content:
Hi Andrea,

the enumerations and typed dimensions are driven by the taxonomy.

That means, the features must be used in the taxonomy and then they will be displayed in Disclosure Management. 

If there should be a taxonomy where some should exist and they are not being displayed, please open a ticket. We will take care of it.

I can also provide an example, upon direct email inquiry, that includes both.

Guido Nachtigall

entry.title: Re: Everything about using SAP Business Transformation center for SDT S/4 HANA Conversion 2023 from
entry.content: Very Good Blog, with lot of information

entry.title: Re: Triger workflow for time off requests
entry.content: @yasaha-ct , our delivery team has configured business rule triggering workflow to check to the leave request status. Based on the status, we have different workflow set and/or notifications send. Hope that this helps.

entry.title: Re: sp_move_file does not find the file with "č" character in filename
entry.content:
the select (select db_property('Collation'), db_property('CharSet'), db_property('NCharCollation'), db_property('NcharCharSet')) returns:

Db Property(collation);Db Property(charset);Db Property(ncharcollation);Db Property(ncharcharset)
UCA;UTF-8;UCA;UTF-8

entry.title: Re: sp_move_file does not find the file with "č" character in filename
entry.content:
In any case, the sp_move_file does not find the file even if I use N'' or use an nchar variable.

CALL xp_write_file('tmp/Testfileč.txt', '123456' ); -- ok

SELECT sp_move_file(N'tmp/Testfileč.txt', N'tmp/Testfileč2.txt'); -- error

entry.title: Re: Possible Issue with SAP Fiori Generator v1.16.0 (ODat...
entry.content:
Hi all.

We encountered the same issue as well. While it is not certain if this is a bug in version 1.16.0, the issue is resolved by downgrading using the following command:

npm install -g @sap/generator-fiori@1.15.7

entry.title: Re: XSLT transformation error
entry.content: (deleted)

entry.title: Re: SAP shutdown
entry.content: No 😞

entry.title: SAP Cloud Foundry - Python and Cloud Connector - TCP
entry.content:
The Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) is a widely used protocol that provides a reliable and ordered delivery of data between applications running on different hosts. It serves as the foundation for many technologies and plays a crucial role in modern IT infrastructure.

SAP Cloud Foundry is the Platform-as-a-service offering on BTP to build all kinds of Apps, Integration and nowadays also AI functionalities. 

In this blog post, we will walk you through the process of establishing connectivity to an on-premises TCP-based system using Python within the Cloud Foundry runtime.

Accessing TCP-Based Resources

Scenario: The objective is to establish a connection to a TCP-based system using Python within SAP Cloud Foundry. Many commonly used systems, such as databases like SAP HANA, PostgreSQL, and MySQL, as well as protocols like SSH and SFTP, rely on TCP for communication.

To achieve this, we will configure the Cloud Connector, a crucial tool for enabling secure communication between SAP Cloud Foundry and on-premises systems. For demonstration purposes, we will use a simple "Hello TCP" server running locally on my computer. The goal is to successfully connect to this server from within SAP Cloud Foundry using the Cloud Connector.

Prerequisite:

Before proceeding with the steps outlined in this guide, it is essential to have an instance of the SAP Cloud Connector installed. While it is possible to install the cloud connector on a server, for the purposes of this demonstration, we will be using a Windows machine. We recommend following the instructions provided in this blog (https://blogs.sap.com/2021/09/05/installation-and-configuration-of-sap-cloud-connector/) to install and configure the cloud connector.

The second requirement is a BTP subaccount with a Data Intelligence cluster. To this Subaccount we will connect the Cloud Connector.

1. Configuration:

The first step is to create a configuration in the Cloud Connector that connects to our subaccount and exposes the TCP resource. For the purpose of this demonstration, I ran a small Node.js server on my Windows machine that outputs "Hello TCP!".

 

 

telnet localhost 4444

Connecting to localhost ...

Hello TCP!

 

To create the configuration, navigate to the admin interface for the cloud connector and create a "Cloud to On-Premise" configuration.

Cloud Connector configuration

In the screenshot above, you can see that I exposed the internal host "localhost" with port 4444 via a virtual host called "virtualhost". This virtual host is the host that we will be requesting from the BTP side. Compared to HTTP resources, we do not need to explicitly expose paths.

BTP Cockpit Cloud Connector Resources

On the BTP end, we can check the cockpit and the connected cloud connectors in the respective menu tab. If you cannot see this tab, you may be missing some roles. It is important to note that we see the LocationID "FELIXLAPTOP", which is an identifier that distinguishes multiple cloud connectors connected to the same subaccount.

2. Create Connectivity Service instance:

To use the Cloud Connector from our runtime environment, we first need a connectivity service instance. Here’s how to do it:

In the BTP Cockpit, go to your desired Subaccount and create a new service instance, as shown below.

 

We use the "Connectivity Service" with the plan "lite". Give the service instance a name, assign it to a space, and click create.

 

The important credentials are stored in a service key. To get these credentials, we create a service key.

 

{
    "clientid": "sb-sampleclientid!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "clientsecret": "****-****-****-****",
    "url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "identityzone": "sample-zone",
    "tenantid": "xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "tenantmode": "dedicated",
    "verificationkey": "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nXXXXXX...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----",
    "xsappname": "sampleappname!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "uaadomain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "credential-type": "binding-secret",
    "onpremise_proxy_host": "connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "onpremise_proxy_http_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_ldap_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_proxy_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_rfc_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port": "20004",
    "token_service_domain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "token_service_url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com"
}

 

We now have a set of credentials, as shown above. The key details are:

  1. OAuth Credentials: These include the "clientid", "clientsecret", and "url". They are used to authenticate when accessing the proxy.

  2. Proxy Details: These include the "onpremise_proxy_host" and the various ports, such as "onpremise_proxy_port", "onpremise_proxy_http_port", and "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port". These details are configured as the proxy to route traffic. The proxy securely tunnels requests through the Cloud Connector to the on-premise destination.

3. Developing a Cloud Foundry App:

Now let’s put this together in a simple Python example to perform an actual TCP request:

The Python script will send a request using Python's standard socket library. We use the connection details provided by the Connectivity Service Service Key. To route the traffic through the Cloud Connector, we specify the proxy configuration, including the host, port, and the proxy-authentication header.

To realize the connection to the Connectivity Proxy, we will be using the sapcloudconnectorpythonsocket library I created for this purpose. This is a seperate dependency and needs to be included into the requirements file. The reason we cannot use standard library's like PySocks to connect to the connectivity service, is the custom authentication flow used.

The actual script looks straightforward:

 

import requests
from sapcloudconnectorpythonsocket import CloudConnectorSocket

# Connectivity Service Service Key
connectivity_service_key = {
    "clientid": "sb-sampleclientid!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "clientsecret": "****-****-****-****",
    "url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "identityzone": "sample-zone",
    "tenantid": "xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "tenantmode": "dedicated",
    "verificationkey": "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nXXXXXX...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----",
    "xsappname": "sampleappname!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "uaadomain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "credential-type": "binding-secret",
    "onpremise_proxy_host": "connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "onpremise_proxy_http_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_ldap_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_proxy_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_rfc_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port": "20004",
    "token_service_domain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "token_service_url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com"
}


# Target Application Details (replace with your app information)
application_host = "virtualhost"
application_port = 4444
location_id = "FELIXLAPTOP"  # Adjust to match your Cloud Connector location

def get_connectivity_service_token(client_id, client_secret, token_service_url):
    """
    Fetches an OAuth token from the SAP Connectivity Service.
    """
    response = requests.post(
        url=token_service_url,
        params={"grant_type": "client_credentials"},
        auth=(client_id, client_secret)
    )

    if response.status_code != 200:
        print(f"Error: {response.status_code} - {response.text}")
        exit(-1)

    return response.json().get("access_token")


def example_tcp_request(host, port, auth_token, location_id, proxy_host, proxy_port):
    """
    Performs an TCP request through SAP Cloud Connector.
    """
    cc_socket = CloudConnectorSocket()
    cc_socket.connect(
        dest_host=host, 
        dest_port=port, 
        proxy_host=proxy_host, 
        proxy_port=proxy_port, 
        token=auth_token,
        location_id=location_id
    )
    
    cc_socket.send(b"")
    
    response = cc_socket.recv(4096)
    return response
    

# Fetch OAuth Token
print("Fetching OAuth token...")
token = get_connectivity_service_token(
    connectivity_service_key["clientid"],
    connectivity_service_key["clientsecret"],
    connectivity_service_key["token_service_url"] + "/oauth/token"
)
print("Token acquired successfully.")

# Perform TCP Request
print("Sending TCP request through SAP Cloud Connector...")
response = example_tcp_request(application_host, application_port, token, location_id, connectivity_service_key["onpremise_proxy_host"], int(connectivity_service_key["onpremise_socks5_proxy_port"]))
print("Response received:")
print(response)

print("Script execution completed.")

 

Let’s break down the steps performed in the script:

  1. Authentication
    The script begins by authenticating with the OAuth endpoint using the details from the service key in

    get_connectivity_service_token. This step retrieves a Bearer Token, which is required to access the Connectivity Proxy.

  2. Establishing the Connection
    Next, the example_tcp_request function is called to send a sample request. This function establishes a connection to the target system via the SAP Cloud Connector. Under the hood, this involves multiple TCP request exchanges, during which the authentication token and location ID are passed. This process results in an open connection through the secure tunnel created by the Cloud Connector.

  3. Using the SOCKS5 Proxy Port
    A key detail to note is that the script uses the SOCKS5 proxy port instead of the standard HTTP proxy port of the Connectivity Service. Looking at the service key of a Connectivity Service instance provides clarity:

    • HTTP Proxy Port: 20003
    • SOCKS5/TCP Proxy Port: 20004

    The SOCKS5 port (20004) is explicitly specified when opening the socket, as it is required for TCP communication in this context.

  4. Sending and Receiving Data
    With the socket successfully opened, the script sends a request to the TCP server. In this example, an empty request body is sent, and the local server should respond with "Hello TCP!" for every request.

To make it deployable on Cloud Foundry, we add a standard manifest.yaml file.

 

---
applications:
- name: cloud_connector_test_task
  memory: 128MB
  buildpack: python_buildpack
  command: python app.py

 

It sets the buildpack to python_buildpack.

Additionally, we include a requirements.txt file in the directory.

 

 

requests
sapcloudconnectorpythonsocket

 

 

Ending up with the following structure:

 

my-python-cloud-connector-app/
│
├── manifest.yaml
├── requirements.txt
├── app.py (or your main Python script)

 

Note: The script we created obviously does not run on our local environment. Because a) we cannot connect to the connectivity service from outside of the BTP and b) the destination URL is not reachable from our local environment. In this blog, I show how to use a hybrid testing setup to develop against backend resources that  are behind the cloud connector.

4. Testing the Application:

Now lets deploy using the following command:

 

cf push cloud_connector_test_task --task

 

You push your script as a task (one-time executable) to the Cloud Foundry space. Then, you start the task using the following command:

 

cf run-task cloud_connector_test_task --command "python app.py" --name example_task
cf logs cloud_connector_test_task --recent

 

This will show the logs from the latest task execution. It may take a few seconds for the task to start and complete.

In the logs, if everything worked correctly, you should see the "Hello TCP!" message from your on-premises machine, confirming that the connection through the Cloud Connector was successful.

 

2024-12-18T23:30:15.23+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Fetching OAuth token...
2024-12-18T23:30:15.38+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Token acquired successfully.
2024-12-18T23:30:15.38+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Sending TCP request through SAP Cloud Connector...
2024-12-18T23:30:15.53+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Response received:
2024-12-18T23:30:15.53+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT b'Hello TCP!'
2024-12-18T23:30:15.53+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Script execution completed.

 

That means we were successful and the script was able to request the local TCP server on my windows machine. Checkout my similar blogpost targeting a example based on HTTP.

Hope you find the content of this blog helpful. Feel free to comment for further clarifications.

entry.title: Closing Stock as per GL Account
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I want a Closing Stock with respect to Closing Stock G/L account with period selection. Data require as per follows :

G/L Acct/BP CodeG/L acct NameLocal Currency - O.B.Local Currency - DebitLocal Currency - CreditLocal Currency - Accu.DebitLocal Currency - Accu.CreditLocal Currency - Balance

entry.title: Re: XSLT transformation error
entry.content:
You didn't choose what to do with the value "C05..." of "subject". If you want to skip it, use <tt:skip/>.

For information, the question title is incorrect: the name XSLT_TOOL is confusing, because you may choose either XSLT or ST (Simple Transformation) language, and in your case, you have chosen ST (see "tt:", it's not "xsl:"). So, your error is about ST, not XSLT. Better use the transaction code alias named STRANS to reduce confusion (or SE80).

For information, you may remove all "workflow." in your ST by using <workflow tt:ref="WORKFLOW"><tt:attribute name="id" value-ref="ID"/>.

For information, you may replace <tag><tt:value ref="REF"/></tag> by <tag tt:value-ref="REF"/>.

Also, please post code and text file as text, not as image.

entry.title: Re: G/L Account wise Inventory Report
entry.content: I also want the same report in SAP B1

entry.title: Re: a xml with two persons details, by using router condi...
entry.content:
Hi @vamsikk 
I believe you are confused, the "Router" step is used to make a decision on which path your flow must take.
But from your explanation it seems what you want to achieve is filtering the data based on certain criteria so that the output is only the Nodelist that is matching your condition.

To achieve this there are multiple options:

A) Filter Step
B) Graphical Message Mapping
C) Groovy Script
D) XSLT Mapping
E) Split + Router

I will cover options A and C as I believe this are the best way to achieve it.
Option A (Simple): Standard functionality of Cloud Integration
Step 1: Store the value for PersonalID in a property
Note: In my case is static but the idea is that you can set the value dynamically

Step 2: Using "Filter" step filter the content based on a property.

Option C (Powerful): Leverage the advantages of Groovy to read and filter the information based on any property, header, or data within the payload itself.

Step 1: Store the value for PersonalID in a property
Step 2: Using "Groovy Script" filter the content based on a property.

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil;
def Message processData(Message message) {
    //Get properties
    def properties = message.getProperties()
    // Read payload
    Reader reader = message.getBody(Reader)
    // Parse payload
    def parsedXml = new XmlSlurper().parse(reader)
    // Get the value for PersonalID you are looking
    def personID = properties.get("personalId")
    // Filter the XML based on this value
    def filteredNode = parsedXml.PersonalDetail.find { it.PersonalID.text() == personID }
    // Serialize
    def filteredXml = XmlUtil.serialize(filteredNode)
    // Return the ouput XML as new Payload
    message.setBody(filteredXml)
    return message;
}

 

Result using "Filter"

Result using Groovy

You can find the iFlow in the following link https://mega.nz/file/qhBQgBKC#Ieyrc_70nkAO_1cOSp9-8zkZ1KdKQZn7LQ7wRhPq4jY

Best regards 🖖🏻
Ricardo

entry.title: Not able to add more than one data source in sapui5 app in cloud foundry
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I have already developed and deployed a SAPUI5 freestyle application in SAP Cloud Foundry, using the SAP C4C API as the data source. Now, I need to consume some data from an on-premise S/4HANA system. However, when I add a second data source to the existing UI5 MTA application, I encounter a 404 error.

I have referred to community discussions and found that similar issues remain unresolved.

Please assist us in identifying what we are missing in adding the second data source. Attached is the project files for your reference.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to restrict access of T-codes involv...
entry.content:
You need to create your Group separately in the Fiori Launchpad designer. Click on the "Groups" tab at the top and create a new group 

Then click on the + at the bottom of the left plane:

From there, click on the new tile, select your new catalog and add your VA01 tile. Both the catalog and the group then need to be added to a PFCG role on the gateway. You will also need to add the transaction itself to a PFCG role on the ECC system.

entry.title: Authorizations for BW modeling - limit to infoarea
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to limit a role in SAP BW and Eclipse BW Modeling in such a way, that the user can only view/change/execute objects that are in a certain infoarea (for now).

In the Authorization Class RS-Business Warehouse I have the following set (among others):

  • S_RS_ADMWB - all activities for all RSADMWBO except CONT_ACT
  • S_RS_ADSO - unlimited for the specific RSINFOAREA
  • S_RS_COMP - unlimited for the specific RSINFOAREA
  • S_RS_COMP - unlimited for it's owner, display and execute for others
  • S_RS_IOBJA - unlimited for the specific RSINFOAREA

Even with just this set, I can see other infoobjects in other infoareas (no other ADSO's though). Preferably, other infoareas would not even be visible. If that's not possible, I'd like to hide all objects in them.

Is this possible?

In the attachment, I have all the authorization objects in my role, under RS. I have tested it with just the upper 5 active but it's the same. Also, I tried to limit it via S_RS_AUTH, where the authorization is limited by the specific infoarea. I also tried with the S_RS_IOBJ but no luck.

After every change, I generated the role and refreshed the buffer in RSOSM + logged off Eclipse.

entry.title: Re: Supplier Personal number(emp no) not copying from one c.code to another c.code
entry.content: How are you extending the vendor to the second company? Personnel Number (PERNR) is a field in table LFB1 (Vendor Master (Company Code)), so needs to be provided for each company code.

Florina

entry.title: SFEC Time Off - Handle contract-end-date with FTE and entitlement basing on calendar year
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have a requirement from client to handle contract employee. Normally they have 14 days per calendar year.

However if FTE < 1 and contract-end-date (CED) in mid-year, the entitlement must be prorated and only able to apply until this CED.

Assuming employee joins from 1st Apr 24 and CED is 31 Mar 25.

  • (1) First, system should only generate 4 days and it valids until 31 Mar 25.
  • (2) Second, HR extends CED to 31 Mar 26, then system should generate 11 days but ONLY valids from 1st Apr 25 to 31 Dec 25.
  • The 4 days generated (1) should not extend, should still 4 days and valids until 31 Mar 25 only.

Similar if emp is on 2 year contract then extend.

I have proposed 2 solutions for them.

Solution 1 (simple one): first geneates 4 days, then when CED is extended, system will regenerate 14 days for WHOLE calendar year.

Solution 2 (complex one): follow exactly what they expect. 

- With solution 2, you know it is complex, because we need to adjust the bookable period as well as how to post entitlement correctly, calculate entitlent is complex too because need to take care FTE as well as CED, and in case emp resigns after CED is extended, etc. 

=> It is the reason why i quote this solution 2 quite high with many mandays required.

=> There are more scenarios will comes and issues will face when coming to implement and testing phase for sure.

 

They are asking to check with sap furum whether someone has covered quite similar requirement and how much efforts you have charged to customers or you may have a better solution, (but must exactly meet the requirement they expect, for my case, the Option 1, they dont want, they want Option 2 but asking why so much efforts required).

Please see the below screenshot to see the exactly requirements they mentioned.

Hope you guys have handled similar requirements and have a reasonable efforts charged to client. 

 

Thanks a lot for your help.

Best regards,

Woody Nguyen

entry.title: Re: Upload Plan data using a ‘Starter Button’ in SAC Story from a File (Excel/CSV)
entry.content: what role do users need to use the upload starter?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori LL19 - Steps for updating SAPUI5 libraries
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" 

Setup the ABAP program by applying notes.

  • 2058624 - sapui5 allow to upload patch via report. This should be already available in your system. It was delivered in SP09 and SP10.
  • 2103131 - SAPUI5 Fixes for update via SMP
  • 2135804 - SAPUI5 patch via SMP - better error handling
  • 2118663 - Install a patch for SAPUI5 runtime libraries. This is just instruction document. Please read the steps.
  • 2150275 - SAPUI5 install patch brings version error     "

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: No output parameters could be determined for document...
entry.content:
Dear @imel_99,

from your screenshot I can see that you have created a new document type for RFQ (ZRFQ).

Could you please make sure you define the Follow-on document categories and types in SSCUI 103895 (Define Document Types for Supplier Quotations)?

In regards to the output, have you set up the Output Parameters for Request for Quotation?

 

best regards,

Marius

entry.title: Re: How to send an email with attachment using outlook sd...
entry.content:
Hello @Ashunitrox , 

I hope I can answer your question.  I am assuming you wanted to handle the base64 to pdf and send it as an attachment using Outlook.

So to do this, you have to save first the base64 content as an "actual file" before you can use it as an attachment.

 First: (I have to create a base64 content, to replicate that I have a b64 data)

(Save the Base64 file as a PDF File)

Once you save the file, you can use it now as an attachment to Outlook:  Add Open Outlook Instance -> add Send Email (Outlook). At the parameter tab, at the bottom there is "Attachments". Add the fullFilePath of your PDF.

Add "Close Outlook Instance" activity. 

I hope this helps. I

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CREATE_SLS giving random result
entry.content:
Entering the order item (SALES_ORDER_ITEMS-REF_ITEM) should have been sufficient.

  • Search for OSS notes for your version
  • Check any modifications to the standard: Enhancements, customer exits, user exits, BadI (BADI_DLV_CREATE_*)

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CREATE_SLS giving random result
entry.content: Do not use Answer to respond to a comment or answer. Answer is intended to be used to propose a solution to the initial question. Use Comment on the initial question or on any answer instead.

entry.title: Re: Data Access Control in the SAP Analytics Cloud on data from an SAP HANA live connection
entry.content:
Hi @ThanhHieuNguyen, 

I haven't done this set-up with an on-prem HANA DB in the background but I assume you can connect to the data through the SAP Cloud Connector. Once you use the secure tunnel of the SAP Cloud Connector you can create new Calculation views in the SAP Business Application Studio and deploy them to an isolated HDI Container and continue with the steps I outlined in my blog post 🙂 
Good luck, Tim

entry.title: Re: Unable to change URL in Target Mapping in Launchpad d...
entry.content: Just to add, the LPD_CUST option is deprecated (it is not available in S/4HANA). You could change the application type to "URL" and maintain the address directly in the launchpad designer.

entry.title: Error - A proper workflow definition could not be found.
entry.content:
When receiving an inbound IDoc we are facing the error below:
Error - A proper workflow definition could not be found.
Message no. APPappl_mm_comp_wfl028

In my case the PO is created, then approved with WF. After approval, IDoc is sent to exsternal system and ORDRSP is received back in S/4, but here we are facing te below error.

I have already checked note 311167 and 2680572 but my case is different. For example, if we change the same PO manually, it will retrigger the WF and go for approval.
So we are facing the error even with a valid WF, but only in case of inbound IDoc for ORDRSP.

By the way, we deactivated the 'Automatic Release of Purchase Order' as was not needed.

Please help me undestand what we are missing.

entry.title: Re: CJI3 - SUBMIT RKPEP003 doesn't work with parameter
entry.content:
With SUBMIT ... WITH field op value, you have to use "=" with PARAMETERS, but you have to use "IN" with SELECT-OPTIONS.

I guess that R_BUDAT is defined as SELECT-OPTIONS.

Read the ABAP documentation or search the forum for more information.

entry.title: Re: While implementing note error occurred coz incorrect cds.how to deimplement without cds correcti
entry.content: Please contact the SAP support if there's an error in the SAP note.

entry.title: Re: how to save mail as draft
entry.content:
Hi @iamanidnya ,

Hope you are well. Besides on Outlook SDK "Save As (Outlook)" activity, this will only allow you to save an email as .msg file. 

There is no functionality yet ( based on my limited knowledge) with regards to saving an Email Draft. What I suggest as workaround is you can use:


"Send Email (Outlook)" - this is to create and send your email message.  Send this to the mailbox you are going to build Outlook rules.

On your outlook client/outlook365 : Add a "Rule" and move the emails coming from or if you have criteria preferred to "Drafts" or Other folder.

It's a workaround for the solution you required.

entry.title: Re: Kit to stock process trigger from EWM using VAS order...
entry.content: Thanks for your help I couldn't download SAP notes. could you give me some explannation for this notes?

entry.title: How to Setup C4C M-user Client Certificate on Cloud Integration hosted on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
This blog will guide you on how to use SSL outbound certificate from C4C system (M-user Certificate) in CPI which is on the cloud foundry.

We may classify the steps as below.

A) Download the C4C client certificate via Administrator.

B) Convert the downloaded M-user certificate to base64 encoded.

C) Create the Service instance and the service key as External certificate (use the C4C M certificate) on SAP BTP.

Step A: Download the C4C client certificate via Administrator

     1. Log in to C4C download the M-user certificate 

     2. Administrator -> Communication Certificates -> Download certificate

Step B) Convert the downloaded M-user certificate to base64 encoded.

  1. Open Certificate.
  2. Click on 'Details' Tab
  3. Click on 'Copy to File...'

     

     4. Click Next

     

     5. Select Base-64 encoded X.509

     

     6. Click Next

     7. Type in File Name

     

     8. Click Next

     

     9. Click on Finish

    10.Right click on 'File Name' and click Edit with Notepad

Step C) Create the Service instance and the service key as an external certificate (use the C4C M certificate) on SAP BTP.

 

Creating Service Instance

  1. Go to SAP BTP cockpit.

  2. Select the subaccount that hosts your SAP Cloud Integration application.

  3. Choose your subaccount, navigate to Services  Service Marketplace, and select Process Integration Runtime.

  4. Choose Create.

      

     5. In the New Instance or Subscription dialog box, Process Integration Runtime is already preselected                   as Service.

     6. Specify the following parameters:

    

 

     7. Choose Next.

     8. Configure instance parameters. Choose how to enter your details via Form.

     

 

     9. Choose Create.

    10. Service Instance Created.

 

Creating Service Key

With this step, you create a service key for the instance created above.

  1. Select the service instance created above .

  2. Under Actions, choose Create Service Key.

     

     3. Enter a name for the service key under Service Key Name. You can use up to 32 characters.

     4. Configure instance parameters. Choose to enter your details via Form.

     5.  Upload M-user client certificate as 'External Certificate' as Key Type, provide a Name and in the External Certificate field add the client certificate in PEM-encoded format (From Step2).

 

    

    6. Choose Create.

 

In the above example, we have used  SAP pre-delivered role ESBMessaging.send while creating a service instance.  We can also configure user-defined roles for such configurations; please refer to blog 

With this we come to conclusion of this blog. Happy Learning!!!

entry.title: Re: New Employee Business Partner Data Model in SAP S/4HANA 2020 On-Premise

entry.title: Re: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content:

Thanks for the post. Looking forward to the versioning and document history release. When can we expect the new features?

entry.title: SuccessFactors Employeetime API canceled timetype for backdated changes
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently working on an integration between SuccessFactors and another Time Attendance system. We are using the employeetime API to fetch leave data from SF.

Consider this scenario: An employee has an approved training time type on December 5th, and the timesheet for that week has already been approved. The API correctly returns the employeetime with the approved time type as training and an externalCode.

Later, the admin or HR team decides to do a backdated change in the time recording for the day before the training ONLY, i.e., December 4th. The API then sends a cancellation for the original training day (December 5th), and create a new record with another external code as approved.

now i have two trainings records for the same day with two different externalCodes, one is approved and another is canceled.

AK

entry.title: Re: migration of company code
entry.content:
Dear Sonia,

Specially as a MM consultant you need to check for all MM relevant Masterdata cleansing and Migration some companies has the different Product supply team so that they assign PO relevant activities to them, or else MM also has to align with Business for all Open POs (should be closed before migration) and rest is to watch rectify all MM relevant customizations in new Company Code.

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: CJI3 - SUBMIT RKPEP003 doesn't work with parameter
entry.content:
Use parameters KS7 for r_budat-low and KS8 for high.

  • Look at (or debug) form SET_DEFAULTS_BUDAT of program RKPEP003

entry.title: proceed button on shopping cart is changing the status to Rejected
entry.content:
Proceed button on shopping cart is changing the status to Rejected when purchasing substitute takeover and hit Proceed button. we have BOTS which will create a shopping cart on behalf of managers or cost center owners.

The Purchasing substitutes maintained on BOT will take over the rejected shopping cart by Operational purchaser  by selecting the "Takeover" button and later will  select the "Proceed" button to re-submit back for approval. but system is changing the status to Release rejected when they select the Proceed button.

entry.title: Re: CJI3 - SUBMIT RKPEP003 doesn't work with parameter

entry.title: Re: Data connection between Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2...
entry.content:
Hello @peter_casper 
we've implemented an import from a Azure Data Lake. As the two colleagues have already mentioned, you have to use two configurations. To help you figure this out faster, than we did, I'll provide you some screenshot of our configuration. If your requirement is to send data there, you can use the identical configration but switch source and target system within the data flow. 


First you need to set up the general connection, that links the CI-DS to the Azure data lake. You need to provide the Data Lake Store name, the access data, the Local directory (on the CI-DS data agent), the remote path prefix within the data lake and the container. 

Next, you need to create the file format. In the configuration part you link it to the connection created beforehand.  


Once you saved it, you have to build the file structure that you are either reading from or writing to the data lake.

This is all configuration, next you only need to create a data flow for it and exchange data. Feel free to ask further questions if needed :). 

Best regards,
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Automatic Batch Determination in Consignment Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

To address the issue of automatic batch determination in consignment issue (CCIS order type) for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you've already taken important steps. Here are a few additional suggestions and checks to ensure everything is in place:

1. **Check Batch Strategy Conditions:**
Make sure that the strategy condition records are maintained correctly. Verify the condition record entries for the combination of order type, plant, and any other criteria you've set up for batch determination. Ensure that the condition types are correct and active for the given order type.

2. **Batch Search Procedure Assignment:**
Double-check that the batch search procedure you've configured is properly assigned to the combination of sales area and sales document type associated with your consignment process.

3. **Availability of Batches:**
Ensure that the relevant materials have batches available in the inventory at the specified plant. The system needs available stock from which it can determine batches.

4. **Batch Determination Configuration:**
Ensure that the batch determination configuration corresponds accurately to your business needs, and all dependencies are addressed:
- All necessary characteristics for determining a batch are properly configured and assigned.
- Check the batch class and characteristics assigned to the materials.

5. **Error Message Analysis:**
Inspect any error messages or logs generated when the batch determination fails. This could provide additional clues as to what might be missing or misconfigured.

6. **SAP Notes and Support Community:**
Check for any relevant SAP Notes or consult the SAP S/4HANA Cloud support community for similar issues or additional insights specific to your setup.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: SAP Work Zone: Fiori app not visible in Content Manag...
entry.content: Hi Wouter, adding the navigation intent in the manifest.json is indeed missing, when generating a Fiori Elements Application based on a ABAP provided OData Service. Thanks for providing the link!

Maintenance plans having scheduling indicator is " Time-Key date".

Please help me out.

entry.content:
Hello Community,

I've published a Standard offline package which includes 'Practice Mode' simulation. When I try to launch the simulation by clicking the 'index.html' file nothing appears in the browser and it's blank. I have tried 'Edge' as well as 'Chrome'  Request your kind help for this issue.

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom Client IDs in SAP Document Informatio...
entry.content: Could you describe the issue you are facing? Is it working in our UI? Did you specify the schema you want to use in addition to the document type?

entry.title: Re: CJI3 - SUBMIT RKPEP003 doesn't work with parameter
entry.content: How is defined so_bldat in your program?

entry.title: "Delivery completed" indicator causes approved Purchase order to be re-sent for approval (Flex. WF)
entry.content:
Hello experts, 
I'd like to ask for your advice. I have the following case with one of my customers:
They use transaction MASS to mark "Delivery completed" indicator in Purchase orders. These POs do not have any GR posted or Invoice attached. These POs are used to charge business trip expenses, service expenses, etc. Invoices are managed without reference to POs. Flexible workflow is in place and each PO is approved by a responsible person when created. After some time, POs are formally closed in SAP by selecting "Delivery completed" indicator. 

After selecting "Delivery completed" indicator in MASS transaction, some of the POs return back to the agent's inbox to be approved again. User's concern is: why some POs need to be re-approved? What is causing this behavior?

Looking at the POs that do not require re-approval and those that do, I do not find any apparent difference. I also do not find any conditions for workflow restart maintained in SPRO under "Flexible workflow", nor Characteristics and Classes. 

Instead, workflow settings seem to have been done in Fiori app "Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders". When I googled a bit, I found out it should be possible to see the workflow restart conditions maintained in this app under "Edit" button, but I do not find it there - maybe I lack certain authorizations? Apart from that, no user exit or BADI has been implemented regarding WF in the system, I've checked that as well. 

My questions would be: Have any of you dealt with the same or similar issue with PO approval restart after modifying "Delivery completed" indicator for certain POs? Where could I find the restart conditions in the mentioned Fiori app (no other apps are there in the system)? Would anyone be able to navigate me how to handle this issue? 

Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: Diff b/w EkkN-AUFPL and EKKN-AUFPL_ORD
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am trying to extract network related routed operation from ekkn table but I find that there are two fields in ekkn which are AUFPL AND AUFPL_ORD. and I find the values in AUFPL_ORD is sometimes blank and sometimes has values. Can someone explain the difference between these 2 fields.

Thanks &regards,

Manogna.

entry.title: Re: UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION - CX_BBP_PD_ABORT
entry.content: Hi Sandeep, is the note still available ?

entry.title: Re: How to find 'DDL source name' from 'DDL View name'?
entry.content:
Open Hana Studio,

TCODE-se11- view --> Enter the DDL SQL view name/DDL Source Name it will show the full details of it.

Regards,
Yarro oruthan.

entry.title: Adding Custom Fields to Migration Objects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi, 

The team tried to add Custom Fields to Migration Objects in Migration Project.

 

Why are the Custom Fields not appearing on the migration template?

Thanks. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Adding Custom Fields to Migration Objects in SAP S/4H...
entry.content:
Thank you for reaching out.

The API currently used for data migration does not support custom fields.

The BOM custom fields shows an API that supports custom fields but is not used for data migration object as it is missing severe data migration structures.

 

To request this functionality, please follow the normal procedure via CIP.

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch KBETR field in price condition using formula routine
entry.content:
Dear RAF,

We have modified code , still we are not getting condition rate in KBETR field. I have attached screenshot .

FORM FRM_KONDI_WERT_918. *{ INSERT GHDK901488 1 * ***********Routine to calculate ZB00 Base price along with pacing rate through calcualtion. by jeet 

data : lt_pack TYPE TABLE OF zpack, wa_pack TYPE zpack.

data : lt_marm TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm type marm.

data : lt_marm1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm1 type marm.

DATA : ZB001 TYPE decfloat34.

DATA : ZB002 TYPE KWERT. DATA : ZB003 TYPE KWERT. data : lv_conversion TYPE f.

SELECT * FROM zpack INTO TABLE lt_pack WHERE matnr = komp-matnr and WERKS = komp-werks and meins = komp-meins.

SORT lt_pack DESCENDING by edate.

SELECT * FROM marm INTO TABLE lt_marm where matnr = komp-matnr and meinh = 'TO'.

READ TABLE lt_marm into wa_marm INDEX 1. READ TABLE lt_pack into wa_pack INDEX 1.

ZB001 = ( wa_marm-umren / wa_marm-umrez ) .

IF KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZPPT'.

ZB002 = ZB001 * KOMV-kbetr .

ZB003 = ZB002 + wa_pack-rate.

XKWERT = ZB003 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 .

ELSEIF

KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZB00'.

ZB002 = KOMV-kbetr.

xkwert = ZB002 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 .

ENDIF. *}

 

INSERT ENDFORM.

entry.title: Understand the World of AI with SAP Learning
entry.content:
Are you also still confused by all the hype around artificial intelligence (AI) and what it is all about? Are you struggling with where to start and how all those new topics fit into the SAP ecosystem? Even many AI skeptics, whom I talk to, are now forced to improve their workflows and systems using AI. But not all problems can be or should be solved by generative AI. For many problems we can still rely on smaller machine learning models. On learning.sap.com, you can find a lot of very good learning content on classical machine learning (ML), AI, and generative AI to make your way through this AI jungle. 

Let me highlight some of the key resources there that can help you get started. 

Exploring the world of AI 

The first course is called Exploring the world of AI where you will get a good understanding of AI and Machine Learning with helpful hands-on examples. If you are a gamer you will enjoy the examples – I promise! You will also learn about different learning techniques such as supervised, unsupervised and reinforcement learning, and you get a basic understanding of neural networks.  

On a side note: a special type of neural network architecture called a transformer is the foundation for large language models (LLMs). The most famous example of an LLM is the model behind ChatGPT. 

”Exploring the World of AI: An agent that learns” 

Generative AI at SAP 

If you are new to the topic of AI, you should check out the second course called Generative AI at SAP. I have observed that a lot of people either overestimate generative AI and think current LLMs will solve all our problems, or they underestimate LLMs because of a couple of wrong responses they received when chatting with a model. If you want to successfully incorporate AI into your business processes, it is important to understand what LLMs are capable of and where their downfalls are. This course will certainly clear up some of the question marks you might have. 

“Generative AI at SAP: Introduction to generative AI” 

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio 

The learning journey Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio is the third resource I can highly recommend. Throughout my travels I have met a lot of people with different roles in the SAP ecosystem and one question I almost always face is: “What is the difference between Joule and Generative AI Hub?“ 

The short answer is: Joule is our AI assistant, embedded in our portfolio and Generative AI Hub is part of our AI solution portfolio (AI Foundation) and gives you a set of tools to build your own generative AI use cases. Whenever Joule or any other generative AI use case at SAP sends a request to an LLM it goes through Generative AI Hub because that is the easiest and safest way to use LLMs in the SAP landscape. This learning journey will give you a better understanding of what SAP offers in AI. 

AI Foundation as part of SAP Business Technology Platform 

Putting AI Ethics into Practice at SAP 

My last recommendation here is the learning journey Putting AI Ethics into Practice at SAP. At SAP the trust of our partners and customers is of utmost importance. This learning journey focuses on how we at SAP are embedding AI into our portfolio by always ensuring our solutions are relevant, reliable, and responsible. This learning journey covers everything about SAP’s Global AI Ethics Framework, our guiding principles for AI ethics and our AI ethics assessment process.  

“Putting AI Ethics into Practice at SAP: Introducing the Main Concepts” 

Additional Developer Content 

If you are a developer and you are looking for hands-on material, more technical explanations and example code, then you should stop by our SAP Developers YouTube channel! Here you can find live streams, video tutorials, an SAP AI playlist and all the sessions of Devtoberfest. For real in-person hands-on sessions, check out the free upcoming SAP CodeJam events near you or host your own SAP CodeJam. You pick the topic; we provide the expert and the system. You can pick between a variety of topics, including three ML and AI CodeJams on machine learning on SAP HANA, generative AI and CAP, and Generative AI Hub and Python. 

Let me know what YOU struggle with when it comes to AI and especially AI at SAP! We will do our best to answer your questions and point you to the right learning materials. Also, I’d love to know which of the four learning resources you enjoyed the most and what your biggest learning was. My highlight was to refresh my knowledge on reinforcement learning with the practical examples given in the course Exploring the world of AI. 

I look forward to hearing from you!

entry.title: Re: Communication error when sending with HTTP, Idoc to H...
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Solved this...

Reason was that there is three application servers and only one had connection settings for the receiver. On frontend run, process used the appl1 and in background appl2 or appl3. Obvious once discovered...

entry.title: Re: How to Activate Tax Number Category Duplication Check for Business Partners in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi I followed exactly the instructions in KBA 2886187 but inserting the business partner with manage business partner master data the system doesn't give me no messages. Is the wrong proess?

entry.title: BTP app integration with Docusign - 401 error within the destination - JWT
entry.content:
We are doing an integration between the docusign and legal insider app, to enable download of specific documents.  We are using oAuth 2.0 with JWT and we are creating a destination. We are currently facing an issue where our application encounters a 401 Unauthorized error when making requests to a destination.

Anyone has any idea how to solve this problem ?

We figure out a workaround, it involves the following steps:

  1. We created a new destination with the authentication type set to "No authentication."
  2. We then routed the API calls through this new destination, handling the authentication logic in the backend by generating the JWT and fetching the access token ourselves.

    But it's not aligned with BTP guidelines, where we should use a destination.  BTP allows this kind of integrations ? it's cloud to cloud.

entry.title: Re: error while installing SAP ABAP netweaver application...
entry.content:
Hi FEZ,

This sometimes is a resource problem. Check that you fulfil all requirements in the Readme, .e.g for Windows:

  • 4 CPUs for Docker Desktop

  • 16GB for Docker Desktop = 32GB total (recommended)

  • 170GB disk for Docker Desktop

If that does not help, you may need to change "ulimits", ie the limits set for each user.

These are stored in the file /etc/security/limits.

You need to check that your limits are set to the recommended settings, by running the following command:

ulimit –a –S

 

cpu (time)=unlimited
fsize (file)=unlimited
data=unlimited
stack=unlimited
rss (memory)=unlimited
core (coredump)=2097151 (which means 1 GByte)
nofiles=32000
threads per process=unlimited
processes per user=unlimited

Best wishes,
Julie Plummer.

entry.title: Default value for buyer (old OMFI) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Public Cloud community,

In CBC the config "Define Default Values for Buyers" is available. However, we don't have user parameters anymore so it does not work via "EVO" anymore to assign a Z-profile to users/buyers.

How to achieve the requirement to create a default Zx rule with a specific setting and assign it to users in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Thank you for your help!

Kind regards,
Jens Koyen

entry.title: Re: Contact person e-mail not updated in ADR6
entry.content:
Hi!
ADR6 is updated when you assign the address in relationship.
You will find detail explanation in this blog:
SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – Address Management ... - SAP Community

BR
Tomasz

entry.title: Credit Management: Is there a TCode for orders released but not invoiced yet?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

As inquired in the topic 😎, I am looking for a TCode where I can list orders released but not yet invoiced, is there a single Tcode which will allow to pull the report, or we need to 2 or more TCodes for this task requiring a kind of reports reconciliation 🤔.

Would appreciate your assistance 😃💪.

Best regards,

Piotr
